perturbation.original_text: NPC shall keep NCB's senior management advised of any material changes in its pricing procedures and standards and of any material exceptions to such procedures and standards which may from time to time be made with respect to particular Merchants. NPC shall not make any such changes or exceptions until its management committee has approved such changes or exceptions after carefully evaluating the relative advantages and disadvantages expected to result from such changes or exceptions.
perturbation.changed_text: NPC shall keep NCB's senior management advised of any material changes in its pricing procedures and standards and of any material exceptions to such procedures and standards which may from time to time be made with respect to particular Merchants. NPC can make such changes or exceptions without the approval of its management committee. This allows for immediate adaptation to market changes, with the only requirement being subsequent notification to NCB.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires NPC's management committee approval for pricing changes, while the modified text removes this requirement, allowing NPC to make changes unilaterally with only subsequent notification to NCB. This contradicts the earlier requirement for mutual agreement on pricing changes, creating uncertainty about the process for modifying pricing standards.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Senior management officials of NPC have met with senior management officials of NCB and have discussed with the NCB officials the pricing procedures and standards used for Merchants as customers for Merchant Processing Services. NPC shall keep NCB's senior management advised of any material changes in its pricing procedures and standards and of any material exceptions to such procedures and standards which may from time to time be made with respect to particular Merchants. NPC can make such changes or exceptions without the approval of its management committee. This allows for immediate adaptation to market changes, with the only requirement being subsequent notification to NCB. If NCB's senior management officials reasonably determine from time to time that any changes are needed in NPC's pricing procedures and standards or in the implementation thereof, then NCB shall so notify NPC in writing, and NPC and NCB shall mutually agree upon, and NPC shall implement appropriate changes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original and contradicted text states, 'If NCB's senior management officials reasonably determine from time to time that any changes are needed in NPC's pricing procedures and standards or in the implementation thereof, then NCB shall so notify NPC in writing, and NPC and NCB shall mutually agree upon, and NPC shall implement appropriate changes.' The changed text eliminates the requirement for mutual agreement, granting NPC unilateral authority to modify pricing standards. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, leading to uncertainty about which process governs pricing changes and hence a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement without penalty and without prejudice to any claims arising prior to termination as follows: (a) Upon the written agreement of both parties. (b) Upon the other party's breach of this Agreement provided the terminating party has given written notice of the breach to the other party specifying the breach, the action necessary to cure the breach and the breaching party has not cured the breach within five business days after notice is given of any failure by NPC to provide any funds required hereunder to be provided by NPC to pay Merchants or within thirty business days after notice is given of any other breach.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement without penalty and without prejudice to any claims arising prior to termination as follows: (a) Upon the written agreement of both parties. (b) NCB may terminate this agreement at any time, for any reason, with 30 days written notice to NPC. NPC cannot terminate this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows either party to terminate for breach, while the modified text allows NCB to terminate at any time for any reason, and expressly forbids NPC from terminating. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the termination rights of the parties and an imbalance in the contract terms, favouring NCB. It contradicts the entire section 7.2 as its modifying the termination rights of NPC, which has become non-existent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement without penalty and without prejudice to any claims arising prior to termination as follows: (a) Upon the written agreement of both parties. (b) NCB may terminate this agreement at any time, for any reason, with 30 days written notice to NPC. NPC cannot terminate this agreement. (c) By NCB immediately upon NPC's voluntary filing of any petition or complaint seeking relief under any federal or state bankruptcy or other debt relief statute or upon an involuntary petition in bankruptcy being filed against NPC if such petition is not dismissed within sixty days after it is filed. (d) By NPC immediately in the event that any agreements between NCB and Visa and/or MasterCard or NCB's membership in either such bankcard association shall be terminated or materially limited which termination or material limitation would impair the ability of NPC to authorize, process or settle merchant bankcard transactions.
perturbation.justification: The modified text fundamentally alters the termination rights outlined in the original text. The original text allows either party to terminate for a breach. The changed text explicitly grants NCB the right to terminate 'at any time, for any reason' while simultaneously forbidding NPC from terminating the agreement, which directly contradicts the original principle of mutual termination rights initially established. The changed text and contradicted text are in the same place in the document, but the changed version introduces a contradiction by negating termination rights for one party.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall bind the parties, their successors and permitted assigns. Neither party shall assign this Agreement or any rights under it except with the prior written consent of the other.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall bind the parties, their successors and permitted assigns. NCB may assign this Agreement or any rights under it to any affiliate or successor without the consent of NPC. NPC is prohibited from assigning this agreement under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires mutual consent for assignment. The modified text allows NCB to assign unilaterally but prohibits NPC from assigning at all. This introduces an in-text contradiction and imbalance regarding assignment rights. The contradiction lies in the original mutual consent and the new, unilateral assignment right granted solely to NCB, affecting the overall agreement's binding nature and assignment clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall bind the parties, their successors and permitted assigns. NCB may assign this Agreement or any rights under it to any affiliate or successor without the consent of NPC. NPC is prohibited from assigning this agreement under any circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The modified text allows NCB to assign the agreement unilaterally while prohibiting NPC from doing so at all. This directly contradicts the original text, which implied a mutual consent for assignment. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, reinforcing the introduced imbalance and thus a strong in-text contradiction exists, satisfying the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
